_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

`design/demo/index.html` is a **TP-Link Wi-Fi captive portal login page**. You can open it locally, but it only works fully if something serves the `/portal/*` API endpoints it calls on load.

## What the page needs

On load, `index.js` POSTs to `/portal/getPortalPageSetting`:

```155:168:design/demo/index.js
Ajax.post(
    '/portal/getPortalPageSetting',
    JSON.stringify({
        "clientMac": clientMac,
        "apMac": apMac,
        "gatewayMac": gatewayMac,
        "ssidName": ssidName,
        "radioId": radioId,
        "vid": vid,
        "originUrl": originUrl
    }),
    function (res) {
        res = JSON.parse(res);
        data = res.result;
```

That response drives which login fields appear (voucher, username/password, SMS, etc.). Without it, you’ll mostly see an empty shell.

The folder contains:
- `index.html`, `index.css`, `index.js`, `jquery.min.js`
- `getPortalPageSetting.json` (sample API response)
- **No** `logo.png` (referenced in HTML but missing)

---

## Option 1: Quick UI preview (simplest)

Serve the folder over HTTP (don’t open via `file://` — AJAX and relative assets behave more reliably over HTTP).

From the demo folder:

```bash
cd /Users/seyi/Downloads/TP/design/demo
python3 -m http.server 8080
```

Then open: [http://localhost:8080/index.html](http://localhost:8080/index.html)

The layout and CSS will load, but the API call will 404 and the form won’t configure itself.

---

## Option 2: Local demo with mocked API (recommended)

You need a server that:

1. Serves the static files from `design/demo`
2. Responds to `POST /portal/getPortalPageSetting` with `getPortalPageSetting.json`

Example with a tiny Node server (save as `server.js` in `design/demo`):

```javascript
const http = require('http');
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const PORT = 8080;
const MIME = { '.html': 'text/html', '.css': 'text/css', '.js': 'application/javascript', '.json': 'application/json', '.png': 'image/png' };

http.createServer((req, res) => {
  if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/portal/getPortalPageSetting') {
    const body = fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'getPortalPageSetting.json'));
    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });
    return res.end(body);
  }

  const file = req.url === '/' ? '/index.html' : req.url;
  const filePath = path.join(__dirname, file.split('?')[0]);
  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
    res.writeHead(404);
    return res.end('Not found');
  }
  res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': MIME[path.extname(filePath)] || 'text/plain' });
  res.end(fs.readFileSync(filePath));
}).listen(PORT, () => console.log(`http://localhost:${PORT}/index.html`));
```

Run:

```bash
cd /Users/seyi/Downloads/TP/design/demo
node server.js
```

Open [http://localhost:8080/index.html](http://localhost:8080/index.html). You should see the login UI configured from the JSON (hotspot mode with voucher, local user, SMS, etc.).

Login won’t fully work unless you also mock:
- `POST /portal/auth`
- `POST /portal/radius/auth`
- `POST /portal/ldap/auth`
- `POST /portal/sendSmsAuthCode`

---

## Option 3: Run on the actual TP-Link portal

This demo is meant to be hosted on the **same host** as the portal API (e.g. Omada controller or AP). The API base URL is implicit — whatever host serves the page:

- Page: `http://192.168.0.1/index.html`
- API: `http://192.168.0.1/portal/getPortalPageSetting`

Upload the portal files through TP-Link’s portal customization UI. The controller provides the real `/portal/*` endpoints.

Optional query params (for realistic behavior):

```
?clientMac=AA-BB-CC-DD-EE-FF&apMac=11-22-33-44-55-66&ssidName=GuestWiFi&originUrl=https://example.com
```

---

## Option 4: Open directly in the browser

Double-click `index.html` or use:

```bash
open /Users/seyi/Downloads/TP/design/demo/index.html
```

This is the least reliable: the `/portal/getPortalPageSetting` request will fail, and some browsers restrict `file://` XHR.

---

## Summary

| Goal | What to do |
|------|------------|
| See layout/CSS only | `python3 -m http.server 8080` in `design/demo` |
| Full UI from sample config | Local server that returns `getPortalPageSetting.json` for `POST /portal/getPortalPageSetting` |
| Real login flow | Deploy on TP-Link portal host, or mock all `/portal/*` endpoints |

If you want, switch to **Agent mode** and I can add a small mock server script to the project for you.

---
